question: When training with DistributedDataParallel (DDP), BatchNorm layers compute statistics independently on each GPU, leading to different normalization on each rank. Convert the model's BatchNorm layers to SyncBatchNorm to synchronize statistics across all processes.
expected_output:
Before conversion:
  bn1: BatchNorm2d
  bn2: BatchNorm2d

After conversion:
  bn1: SyncBatchNorm
  bn2: SyncBatchNorm
template:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class SimpleModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 64, 3)
        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(64)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(64, 128, 3)
        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(128)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(128 * 26 * 26, 10)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.bn1(self.conv1(x))
        x = self.bn2(self.conv2(x))
        return x

model = SimpleModel()
print("Before conversion:")
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    if isinstance(module, nn.BatchNorm2d):
        print(f"  {name}: {type(module).__name__}")

# Convert BatchNorm to SyncBatchNorm for DDP
???

print("\nAfter conversion:")
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    if isinstance(module, (nn.BatchNorm2d, nn.SyncBatchNorm)):
        print(f"  {name}: {type(module).__name__}")
